prevalence.source: SBR + Latin America SLE epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis increasing with improved access to rheumatology and labs

vulnerablePopulations: Afro-descendant and mixed ancestry populations (higher severity in some cohorts)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ACR/EULAR classification criteria supported by ANA positivity, immunologic markers (anti-dsDNA, anti-Sm), complement, and clinical organ involvement; diagnosis is clinical

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; outcomes depend on early nephritis detection and access to immunosuppression

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ANA and basic immunology tests available; complement/autoantibody panels and renal biopsy access vary by region
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydroxychloroquine for most; corticosteroids and immunosuppressants for organ disease; biologics in selected cases; pregnancy planning and nephritis management in specialist centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access to rheumatology and renal biopsy, infection risk with immunosuppression, and adherence challenges
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS access to core therapies (HCQ, steroids) and specialized centers in major cities
